response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. To calculate how many bottles she needs for a leap year, we first determine how many days there are in a leap year. A leap year has 366 days. Since she uses shampoo every other day, she will use it 366 / 2 = 183 times. Each bottle contains 14 ounces of shampoo, and she uses 1/4 ounce per use. 

To find out how many bottles she needs, we calculate the total amount of shampoo she will use: 183 * (1/4) = 45.75 ounces. Since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, she needs to round up to the next whole number. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so we divide 45.75 by 14 to find how many bottles she needs: 45.75 / 14 ≈ 3.267. She needs 4 bottles to cover her shampoo usage.

[amount of shampoo used per use] and [number of days in a leap year] are key factors in determining how many bottles Brittany needs.
